title: Sea Peoples — Aegean to the Nile Delta

intro.title: The Year of the Burning Coasts
intro.body: Egyptian reliefs at Medinet Habu record Ramesses III's defence against a confederation of Sea Peoples — Peleset, Tjekker, Shekelesh, Denyen, Weshesh — whose passage left every major coastal city from Ugarit to the Delta burned in a single generation.

stops.name: Aegean (Mycenae)
stops.note: Origin theatre. Linear B tablets at Pylos record coastal watch postings before the palace's destruction c. 1180 BCE.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Lion gate and citadel above a plain, smoke rising from a distant palace.

stops.name: Ugarit
stops.note: Letter RS 20.18: 'My father, the enemy ships are already here, they have set fire to my towns.' Ugarit fell c. 1190-1185 BCE.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Coastal city in flames, fleet of crested-helmet warriors landing from low galleys.

stops.name: Cyprus (Enkomi)
stops.note: Enkomi shows a destruction layer dated c. 1190 BCE consistent with Sea Peoples passage; the site was resettled but never recovered.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Walled town on a low coast, breach in the wall, raiders within.

stops.name: Levantine coast (Ashkelon)
stops.note: Peleset (Philistines) settled the southern Levant after the raids; Ashkelon, Ashdod, Ekron, Gath, Gaza became their pentapolis.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Low dunes inland from a sandy beach, longhouses being raised by feathered-helmet warriors.

stops.name: Battle of the Delta
stops.note: Year 8 of Ramesses III. The Medinet Habu reliefs depict the land and sea battle in which the Sea Peoples confederation was broken.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Delta river battle with reed-prowed Egyptian ships engaging crested-helmet warriors; archers on the bank.
